methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

private.name: Matic Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Shinkei
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Whisper Aero is a Series A-stage consumer tech company founded by Mark Moore and Ian Villa that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three private-stage comparables—Matic Robotics, Shinkei, and Daylight Computer—given the absence of public trading comps in this segment.

The stage-progression method, the applicable triangulation approach given data constraints, anchors to the $40M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage step-ups. This yields an implied valuation of $80M. The method assumes Whisper Aero's capital deployment and progress are broadly representative of frontier-tech Series A trajectory.

The triangulated valuation range is $68M (low) to $92M (high), with a median of $80M. The spread reflects inherent uncertainty in frontier-tech valuation: single-method reliance, limited public market precedent, and dependence on private comp multiples that themselves carry significant variance. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peer set, time-to-next-funding runway, and product-market validation metrics not present in available data. A broader comp set or revenue/burn-rate inputs would meaningfully improve conviction in this range.
